title: pseudephedranthus fragrans
intro: Pseudephedranthus fragrans, also known as False Ephedra, is a flowering plant species in the family Annonaceae. It is native to tropical Africa and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antipyretic, analgesic, and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant is rich in bioactive compounds such as alkaloids and terpenoids, which have been shown to have health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and protecting against oxidative stress and inflammation. The herbal preparations of Pseudephedranthus fragrans include infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which are often used to treat various health conditions, including respiratory problems, fever, and skin conditions.
